hyperedges.label: 3D Game Engines Sharing the Wall-Clock Architecture

nodes.label: Static-export Constraints
nodes.rationale: No cookies, middleware, rewrites, request-reading route handlers, next/image default loader, or dynamicParams:true; every dynamic route needs generateStaticParams. A build error is the main signal a constraint was broken.

nodes.label: KidsCode App
nodes.rationale: Mobile-friendly web app teaching kids to think like programmers via mini-games (EN + Bahasa Indonesia); real code is the finale after logic foundations. Everything free, no paywall.

nodes.label: Firebase Free (Spark) Plan Constraint
nodes.rationale: Owner wanted free hosting; the Spark plan serves static assets only — SSR requires the paid Blaze plan — which forced the whole app to become a static export with client-only Firebase SDKs.

nodes.label: Static Export Architecture
nodes.rationale: next.config.ts output:'export', images.unoptimized; no server, cookies, middleware, /api routes, or dynamicParams. Every dynamic route needs generateStaticParams. A build error is the main signal a constraint was broken.

nodes.label: Firebase Authentication (Google Sign-in)
nodes.rationale: Replaced NextAuth/Prisma/Postgres because a static export has no server; client SDK signInWithPopup, optional sign-in.

nodes.label: Cloud Firestore Progress Sync (users/{uid})
nodes.rationale: users/{uid} = { completed: string[] } via arrayUnion in src/lib/cloudProgress.ts; replaces the old /api/me and /api/progress server routes; owner-only access enforced by firestore.rules.

nodes.label: Sequential Progression Lock
nodes.rationale: After pricing removal the only lock reason is 'progress' (previous world unfinished) — gentle pacing so the map reads like a learning path; no premium paywall remains.

nodes.label: Master Account Testing Bypass
nodes.rationale: Re-added after the pricing removal so testers can reach any game instantly: worldLockReason returns null for MASTER_EMAILS accounts, bypassing sequential progression. Requires real Firebase sign-in; profile shows a Master badge.

nodes.label: Per-Account localStorage Progress Isolation
nodes.rationale: Progress keys are scoped per account (owner-scope key is now the Firebase uid) so a second account on the same browser can never inherit the first one's progress; anonymous progress is adopted once at sign-in then cleared.

nodes.label: Wall-Clock Outcome Architecture Rule
nodes.rationale: Game outcomes are committed by wall-clock setTimeouts in the React layer; useFrame renders poses as pure functions of performance.now() elapsed. Never gate outcomes on rAF — throttled/background tabs suspend it and would wedge the game.

nodes.label: Client-Side i18n (LocaleProvider)
nodes.rationale: next-intl middleware and the src/i18n routing layer were deleted (no server in a static export); LocaleProvider imports both message bundles and swaps them in-memory, locale stored in localStorage 'kidscode-locale', clean URLs with no /en /id prefix.

nodes.label: Config-Optional Firebase
nodes.rationale: Without NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_* env vars sign-in is hidden and the app runs anonymous localStorage-only — so most work is verifiable in dev without credentials; only the live Auth/Firestore path needs a real project.

nodes.label: 3D Games Revamp (three.js + react-three-fiber)
nodes.rationale: Owner asked for 3D + animation, fun for ~5-year-olds, minimal reading, no typing; client-only canvas works in the static export. 1 world x 3 levels x 5 games = 15 games with an in-level difficulty ramp.

nodes.label: Precise Commands (RobotGameView)
nodes.rationale: 3D tile board with a hopping voxel Robo; bump-shake on crash, wobble on miss, confetti on win. The final 6x4 labyrinth's 7-block limit deliberately forces the x2/x3 repeat blocks — the kid's first taste of loops.

nodes.label: Pattern Power (PatternGameView)
nodes.rationale: 3D 'pattern parade' — emoji sprites bob on pedestals, picked answer drops in with a bounce (right: confetti + wave; wrong: head-shake + tumble). Difficulty ramp AB -> AAB -> ABB -> ABC -> ABAC teaches pattern recognition and abstraction.

nodes.label: Robo Says (MemoryGameView, memory game kind)
nodes.rationale: Simon-style watch-and-repeat; NEW 'memory' game kind in curriculum/types.ts plus a GameHost case. Zero-reading design: the demo IS the instruction, emoji-only hints and progress dots; a wrong tap just replays the song. Ramp 2 pads/2 beats -> 4 pads/6 beats.

nodes.label: XP and Ranks System
nodes.rationale: +10 XP per game; 6 ranks at 30 XP each (Egg -> True Programmer) in src/lib/ranks.ts. Convention: thresholds spread evenly so the top rank lands on the final game — must be rescaled whenever the game count changes.

nodes.label: Data-Driven Curriculum (worlds/ + auto-numbering)
nodes.rationale: Games are data-driven from src/lib/curriculum/ — one file per world in worlds/, auto-numbered by index.ts from position (no manual numbering). Legacy engines (order/choice/debug/CodeRunner) were deliberately kept though unused so revamps can reuse them; deleted worlds recoverable at git ede158e.

nodes.label: CodeRunner (sandboxed JS engine)
nodes.rationale: Sandboxed JS with say() for the real-code finale; currently unused after the trim. Known risk: an output-less while(true) can freeze the tab — planned fix is moving execution into a Web Worker with a timeout.

nodes.label: Prepaid Pass Pricing (removed)
nodes.rationale: Historical: subscriptions were replaced by one-time prepaid passes because auto-renew billing is card-only in Indonesia while QRIS/e-wallet/VA are one-shot rails; buying while active extended currentPeriodEnd. The whole pricing system was later removed in the Firebase revamp (#12) — everything is free now.

nodes.label: In-App Invoices (removed)
nodes.rationale: Historical: purchase detail was delivered in-product instead of email — better fit than SMTP for a web app (no domain, no deliverability concerns, always available); replaced a prior nodemailer + renewal-reminder cron approach. Deleted with pricing in #12.

nodes.label: Firebase Deploy Workflow (npm run deploy)
nodes.rationale: npm run deploy = next build && firebase deploy; next build emits out/, firebase.json points Hosting at it with cleanUrls for prefix-less routes and ships the Firestore rules.

nodes.label: NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_* Environment Config
nodes.rationale: Public Firebase web config values ship to the browser by design; access is guarded by Firestore security rules, not by keeping the keys secret.

nodes.label: Firestore Security Rules (owner-only users/{uid})
nodes.rationale: users/{uid} owner-only read/write so a user can only touch their own progress document — this is the actual access control layer, since the Firebase config is public.

nodes.label: Runner Hardening TODO (Web Worker)
nodes.rationale: The code runner guards against output floods, but an output-less while(true) can still freeze the tab; the fix is moving execution into a Web Worker with a timeout.
